title: 10 best herbal teas for migraine
intro: Herbal teas, such as chamomile, feverfew, and ginger, offer natural relief for migraines. Chamomile, with its calming properties, soothes the mind and promotes relaxation. Feverfew, known for its anti-inflammatory effects, helps alleviate pain and reduce the frequency of migraines. Ginger tea eases nausea and vomiting, common symptoms associated with migraines, while providing a soothing warmth. When suffering from a migraine, consider these herbal teas as a gentle, caffeine-free alternative to ease your discomfort.

teas.tea_desc: Feverfew, a plant in the daisy family, produces a tea that benefits migraine relief due to its active compounds, including parthenolide. Parthenolide inhibits the release of substances that widen blood vessels in the brain, a known cause of migraines. By doing so, feverfew tea helps restore normal blood flow and reduce inflammation. Additionally, feverfew tea prevents platelet aggregation, minimizing the risk of blood clots that could induce or worsen migraines. Finally, feverfew tea boasts antispasmodic properties that relax constricted muscles around blood vessels, further alleviating migraine symptoms.
tea_parts: Leaves: Contain parthenolide, a compound shown to inhibit the release of chemicals that cause migraines.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, helping reduce inflammation and providing relief from migraines.
Buds: High in parthenolide and other compounds, buds can ease spasms and reduce pain associated with migraines.
Seeds: Contain essential oils, which can help relax muscles and alleviate tension, a common cause of migraines.
tea_constituents: Parthenolide: Inhibits the release of prostaglandins and serotonin, which are inflammatory substances that can cause migraines.
Tanacetin: A sesquiterpene lactone that helps to reduce inflammation and relieve migraine symptoms.
Camphor: A natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that can help to alleviate migraine pain.
Artabsin: A sesquiterpene lactone that helps to reduce muscle spasms and alleviate tension headaches.
Sesquiterpene lactones: A group of compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective at reducing the severity and frequency of migraines.
tea_recipe: Gather 2-3 fresh feverfew leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried feverfew leaves.
Rinse the fresh leaves under cold water, then chop them finely.
Place the leaves in a teapot and add 250ml (8oz) of boiling water.
Steep the tea for 5-10 minutes, then strain it into a cup.
Drink the tea slowly, preferably with a meal, to alleviate migraine symptoms.

teas.tea_desc: Butterbur tea contains petasins, chemical compounds with proven anti-inflammatory and muscle-relaxing properties. These petasins inhibit the production of leukotrienes and prostaglandins, substances that cause blood vessel dilation and inflammation in the brain, leading to migraine attacks. Double-blind studies demonstrate that butterbur extract reduces migraine frequency and severity by up to 50% in some individuals. Moreover, the American Headache Society and the American Academy of Neurology recognize butterbur as an effective preventive treatment for migraines. Its consumption leads to a significant decrease in migraine symptomatology.
tea_parts: Leaves: Contain petasins, anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce migraine frequency and intensity.
Rhyzomes: Rich in essential oils and nutrients, contributing to the plant's effectiveness in alleviating migraine symptoms.
Buds: High in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, providing additional relief for migraines.
Flowers: Contain valuable phytochemicals that aid in soothing and calming the nervous system, therefore reducing migraine pain.
Roots: Provide a source of essential nutrients and bioactive compounds, enhancing the therapeutic effects of butterbur tea for migraines.
tea_constituents: Petasin: Relaxes smooth muscle tissue and reduces inflammation, which can help prevent migraines.
Isopetasin: Acts as a natural analgesic and antispasmodic, providing relief from migraine symptoms.
Phenylethanoid glycosides: Possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate migraine-related inflammation.
Flavonoids: Provide neuroprotective effects by scavenging free radicals and stabilizing cell membranes, offering additional relief for migraines.
tea_recipe: Measure - Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down to 212 degrees Fahrenheit.
Prepare - Cut 2 teaspoons of dried butterbur root into small pieces.
Steep - Place the butterbur pieces in a tea infuser and steep in the hot water for 10 minutes.
Strain - Remove the tea infuser and discard the butterbur pieces.
Serve - Enjoy the butterbur tea while it's still warm, up to 3 times a day.

teas.tea_desc: Peppermint tea aids migraine relief due to its menthol content, a known natural analgesic and muscle relaxant. This compound works by improving blood flow, reducing tightness in cranial muscles, and diminishing sensitivity to pain. Moreover, menthol boasts anti-inflammatory and calming qualities that further help alleviate migraine symptoms. By sipping peppermint tea, you harness these soothing properties, harnessing a holistic and caffeine-free solution for potential migraine relief. Ultimately, peppermint tea's therapeutic benefits stem from its organic compounds, providing a non-invasive and side-effect-light approach compared to conventional medicines.
tea_parts: Leaves: Contain menthol and antimigraine properties, making them effective in reducing migraine symptoms.
Flowers: Rich in volatile oils and antioxidants, known to alleviate pain and inflammation associated with migraines.
Seeds: Packed with essential oils and rosmarinic acid, showing potential in relieving spasms and migraine pain.
Buds: Contain high levels of flavonoids and polyphenols, offering anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving benefits for migraine sufferers.
tea_constituents: Menthol: A natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce migraine pain and inflammation.
Limonene: A terpene that acts as a natural stress reliever and mood elevator, helping to alleviate migraine-induced anxiety and depression.
Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic acid with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with migraines.
Luteolin-7-O-glucoside: A flavonoid that acts as a natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic compound, helping to alleviate migraine symptoms.
Eriocitrin: A flavonoid that exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation linked to migraines.
Hesperidin: A flavanone with vasodilatory properties that helps improve blood flow and reduces the risk of migraines.
Naringin: A flavanone that exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate migraine-related oxidative stress and inflammation.
tea_recipe: Boil 8 ounces of water to 212 degrees Fahrenheit, the optimal temperature for releasing peppermint's therapeutic oils.
Crush 1 tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves to increase the release of menthol, a proven anti-inflammatory agent.
Steep the crushed peppermint leaves in boiling water for 5-10 minutes to extract the medicinal compounds.
Strain the liquid to remove the spent peppermint leaves, eliminating any potential bitterness.
Sip the hot peppermint tea slowly, allowing the soothing warmth and calming properties to alleviate your migraine symptoms.

teas.tea_desc: Ginger, a key ingredient in tea for migraines, contains compounds that inhibit prostaglandin synthesis, reducing inflammation and pain. Gingerols and shogaols found in ginger exhibit strong anti-inflammatory properties similar to NSAIDs and COX-2 inhibitors, providing relief without the side effects. A 2014 study in the Phytotherapy Research journal reveals ginger's ability to match sumatriptan in migraine alleviation while causing fewer adverse reactions. Furthermore, ginger's antioxidative and antiemetic effects make it an ideal natural remedy to soothe migraine symptoms. Consequently, drinking ginger tea delivers these beneficial compounds directly, easing pain and inflammation associated with migraines.
tea_parts: Rhizomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, helping reduce oxidative stress linked to migraines.
Buds: High in essential oils, providing soothing effects for migraine relief.
Seeds: Possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, aiding in pain management.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and ease migraine tension.
tea_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols found in ginger tea possess strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce prostaglandin synthesis, a key contributor to migraine pain.
Shogaols: These compounds show potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, helping alleviate migraine-associated pain and inflammation.
Paradols: Paradols in ginger tea exhibit notable anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, contributing to the reduction of oxidative stress and inflammation in migraines.
Zingerone: Zingerone has been shown to inhibit the release of neuropeptides involved in migraine development, lessening the severity and frequency of migraine attacks.
tea_recipe: Peel and grate 1 tablespoon of fresh ginger root.
Boil 4 cups of water, then add grated ginger, reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes.
Remove from heat, optionally add 1 teaspoon of honey and 1 tablespoon of lemon juice.
Strain the tea to remove ginger pieces.
Serve brewed ginger tea while still warm to aid in relieving migraine symptoms.

teas.tea_desc: Willow tea provides relief from migraines due to its salicin content, a compound similar to aspirin. This natural anti-inflammatory agent found in willow bark works by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, chemicals that cause inflammation and pain in blood vessels surrounding the brain. By reducing inflammation, willow tea alleviates the constriction and dilation of blood vessels, which are primary factors leading to migraine headaches. Furthermore, willow bark has been proven to possess fever-reducing and analgesic properties, making it an effective herbal remedy for migraine relief. Lastly, willow tea's pain-relieving abilities stem from its ability to block the production of inflammatory substances, effectively reducing the symptoms associated with migraines.
tea_parts: Bark: The bark of the willow tea plant contains salicin, which is a natural pain reliever and is effective in treating migraines.
Leaves: The leaves of the willow tea plant are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and vasoactive properties, making them useful in treating migraines.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the willow tea plant are a potent source of salicylic acid, a proven pain-reliever and anti-inflammatory agent that is helpful in managing migraines.
tea_constituents: Salicin: A natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce swelling and pain in blood vessels, which is associated with migraines.
Flavonoids: These antioxidants have been shown to improve cerebral blood flow and provide relief from migraine symptoms.
Tannins: Known for their astringent properties, tannins help to narrow blood vessels, reducing the risk of migraine-related swelling.
Phenolic acids: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can alleviate pain and discomfort caused by migraines.
tea_recipe: Gather 2-3 willow bark branches, peel off the outer bark, and cut the inner bark into small pieces (10-15 minutes).
Place 1 tablespoon of willow bark pieces in a pot, add 2 cups of water, and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, simmer for 10-15 minutes, then remove from heat.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the willow bark pieces.
Drink the willow tea while warm, up to 3 times a day for migraine relief.

teas.tea_desc: Valerian tea aids in migraine relief due to its active compounds, including valerenic acid and valepotriates, which possess sedative and analgesic properties. These components work by binding to specific receptors in the brain, reducing neuronal excitability, and alleviating pain. Valerian tea also has anti-inflammatory effects that help minimize swelling in blood vessels in the head, a common cause of migraines. Moreover, it promotes relaxation and stress reduction, addressing one of the common triggers of migraines. Lastly, valerian tea's soothing qualities help improve sleep quality, reducing the frequency and intensity of migraines.
tea_parts: Roots: Contain active compounds that help relax muscles and calm nerves, making them effective at reducing migraine symptoms.
Rhizomes: Have sedative properties that aid in promoting sleep and reducing anxiety, commonly used as a natural remedy for migraine relief.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, flowers help reduce inflammation and provide a soothing effect that can alleviate the pain and discomfort caused by migraines.
Leaves: Contain volatile oils known to help relax the central nervous system, making them useful in treating the frequency and severity of migraines.
Seeds: Packed with essential oils and antioxidants, seeds offer a mild sedative effect that can relieve tension and reduce the onset of migraines.
tea_constituents: Valerenic acid: Interacts with GABA receptors in the brain to reduce anxiety and promote sleep, helping to alleviate migraine symptoms.
Valepotriates: Possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with migraines.
Flavanoids: Exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to migraines.
Essential oils: Contain sedative and analgesic properties, helping to relieve tension, reduce pain, and promote relaxation.
GABA: A neurotransmitter that helps regulate nerve cells and reduce excitability, potentially helping to alleviate migraine symptoms.
tea_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a teapot or saucepan for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried valerian root to a teapot or infuser.
Pour the boiling water over the valerian root and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the used valerian root.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day as needed.

teas.tea_desc: Lavender tea aids in alleviating migraines due to its established sedative and soothing properties. The active compounds in lavender, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that target pain and inflammation associated with migraines. Moreover, lavender tea demonstrates anxiolytic properties, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, consequently diminishing the frequency and intensity of migraines. Additionally, lavender's calming scent, when inhaled, stimulates the limbic system, impacting the brain's regulation of stress and pain, further alleviating migraine symptoms. Lastly, lavender tea's antioxidant and antispasmodic qualities contribute to its efficacy in managing migraines by scavenging free radicals and relieving muscle tension, respectively.
tea_parts: Flowers: Contain high levels of relaxing essential oils, making them ideal for soothing migraines.
Leaves: Rich in calming nutrients and antioxidants, leaves help reduce migraine-related inflammation.
Buds: Packed with powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, buds can alleviate migraine symptoms.
Stems: Contain medicinal compounds that can help reduce muscle tension, a common migraine trigger.
tea_constituents: Linalool: A terpene alcohol known for its relaxant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping reduce tension and soothe swelling in the brain's blood vessels during migraines.
Acetate: A compound that acts as a natural sedative, aiding in the reduction of anxiety and promoting better sleep, both crucial in managing migraines.
Eucalyptol: A compound shown to improve circulation, alleviating restricted blood flow that can contribute to migraine pain.
Cineole: A monoterpene with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce inflammation and pain in the head.
Limonene: A compound that elevates mood and reduces stress, both of which can help prevent migraines.
Beta-caryophyllene: A sesquiterpene with potent anti-inflammatory properties, that can help minimize the inflammation involved in migraine attacks.
tea_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water to 212°F (100°C) for 1-2 minutes, releasing steam for purification.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried organic culinary-grade lavender flowers to a tea infuser or reusable tea bag.
Place the tea infuser in the boiled water, cover the teacup, and let it steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.
Remove the tea infuser to avoid over-extracting the herb, which can lead to bitterness.
Enjoy your hot soothing lavender tea made with organic ingredients, sip by sip, for natural migraine relief.

teas.tea_desc: Linden tea brings immediate relief to migraine sufferers because it contains antispasmodic agents, which relax constricted blood vessels in the head. This herbal tea, derived from the linden tree's flowers, boasts anti-inflammatory properties that alleviate pain by reducing swelling in surrounding tissues. Linden tea's soothing effect on the nervous system also helps to lower stress levels, a common migraine trigger. Furthermore, this potent tea acts as a natural sedative, promoting restorative sleep that aids in recovery from migraines. Consequently, drinking linden tea provides confirmed therapeutic benefits for those seeking respite from the debilitating effects of migraines.
tea_parts: Leaves: Contain compounds that help relax blood vessels and reduce inflammation, making them effective in treating migraines.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and volatile oils that provide soothing effects on the nervous system, helping to alleviate symptoms of migraines.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids and flavonoids, promoting vascular health and reducing the frequency and intensity of migraines.
tea_constituents: Flavonoids: These antioxidants reduce inflammation and help alleviate symptoms of migraines by relaxing blood vessels and reducing swelling.
Manganese: An essential nutrient that regulates brain function and neurotransmitter activity, aiding in the relief of migraines.
Tannins: Astringent compounds that help reduce inflammation, effectively easing pain associated with migraines.
Quercetin: A potent flavonoid known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help manage symptoms of migraines.
Kaempferol: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective effects, providing relief and potentially preventing migraine attacks.
Rutin: A bioflavonoid that reinforces blood vessels, improves circulation, and alleviates symptoms of migraines.
tea_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried linden flowers, depending on desired strength.
Place the linden flowers in a tea infuser or teapot.
Boil water and pour it over the linden flowers, allowing them to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the infuser or strain the tea to discard the linden flowers.
Sip the warm linden tea to alleviate migraine symptoms.

teas.tea_desc: Skullcap tea brings immediate relief to migraine sufferers due to its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, derived from bioactive compounds like scutellarin. This herbal tea, native to North America, contains active ingredients that directly target the underlying causes of migraines, such as vasodilation and nerve cell hyperexcitability. By reducing inflammation in the cranial blood vessels, the tea alleviates pressure and pain in the head. Furthermore, drinking skullcap tea helps restore neurotransmitter balance, promoting relaxation and stress reduction, both of which contribute to the prevention of migraine attacks. These documented benefits make skullcap tea an ideal remedy for managing the symptoms of migraines and improving overall quality of life.
tea_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and flavonoids, which are known to help reduce inflammation and provide relief from migraine symptoms.
Flowers: Rich in essential oils, which can help relax muscles, reduce tension, and alleviate migraine pain.
Buds: Packed with nutrients and antioxidants, promoting circulation and reducing inflammation, effectively easing migraine pain.
Stems: Contain beneficial compounds, providing support for the nervous system, and helping to alleviate the severity and frequency of migraine attacks.
Seeds: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease pressure in the head, bringing relief from migraines.
tea_constituents: Flavone: Acts as a natural anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce the inflammation associated with migraines.
Flavonoid: Possesses antioxidant properties that protect against oxidative stress, a possible factor in migraines.
Phenolic Acid: Helps in relieving pain as it has natural analgesic effects.
Tannin: Known for its astringent properties, it helps in reducing swelling and soothing irritated nerves.
Saponin: Has been shown to act as a sedative, helping to manage stress and promote relaxation, reducing the likelihood of migraines.
Essential Oils: Comprise of various terpenes, including germacrone, which has anti-inflammatory and anticonvulsant effects, beneficial for migraine relief.
tea_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or pot for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried skullcap herb to a tea infuser or strainer in a cup.
Pour the boiling water over the skullcap herb in the cup and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strainer with the spent herb and discard it.
Drink the skullcap tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day as needed.

teas.tea_desc: Passionflower tea provides relief from migraines due to its documented sedative and analgesic properties. This natural remedy contains flavonoids, such as vitexin and isovitexin, which inhibit the production of inflammatory compounds, thereby reducing pain and swelling in blood vessels in the brain. Moreover, passionflower tea affects the brain's GABA receptors, promoting relaxation, alleviating anxiety, and ultimately minimizing the frequency and intensity of migraine attacks. Clinical studies confirm that this herbal infusion boasts significant pharmacological activities, including anticonvulsant, antispasmodic, and anxiolytic effects, contributing to its efficacy in managing migraines. Consequently, passionflower tea's unique phytochemical composition and well-established physiological impacts make it a valuable tool in addressing the symptoms and causes of migraines.
tea_parts: Leaves: Contain flavonoids that have shown to help reduce inflammation and relax tension, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe nerves and reduce migraine pain.
Stems: Contain alkaloids that act as natural relaxants, helping to reduce muscle tension and ease migraine pain.
Seeds: High in fatty acids and linoleic acid, which can help reduce inflammation and promote blood flow, alleviating migraine symptoms.
tea_constituents: Flavonoids: These antioxidants reduce inflammation and help alleviate migraine symptoms by inhibiting the release of certain chemicals that cause pain and inflammation in the brain.
Harman alkaloids: These compounds have been shown to act as natural sedatives, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, indirectly alleviating tension-related migraines.
Chrysin: A flavonoid that has been suggested to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help manage the pain and swelling associated with migraines.
Vitexin: This flavonoid may have an impact on the neurotransmitter serotonin levels, which are often imbalanced during migraine attacks, thus providing relief.
Orientin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory effects, orientin can help lessen the effects of inflammation, a common element in migraine-related discomfort.
Isoorientin: Similar to orientin, isoorientin exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that can help manage migraine-associated swelling and pain.
tea_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water to a rolling boil, which takes about 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried passionflower leaves to the boiling water.
Steep the passionflower leaves in the hot water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the passionflower tea into a cup, discarding the spent leaves.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day, as needed for migraine relief.
